© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Key Takeaways:
• We’re going to now talk about the doing the pure evergreen weekly webinar model.
One of my Platinum mastermind members is using this very effectively.
• Each week you gather leads and they all go into a list. On Monday they go into a
sequence for your launch.
• That week all the new leads coming in would start the sequence the following
Monday. This results in having two launches happening at the same time, and
you’ll know within 10-15 days whether the traffic you bought is converting.
• You need to decide if you want this completely 100% automated, or you could send
the emails out as broadcasts and watch over things a bit. This is up to you, but I
suggest you do some manual work the first few times and then shift to automating
it completely.
• Watch the traffic, metrics, broadcasts, comments, etc. The benefit is you can do a
lot of testing and see what works better each week.
• You should also start at a monthly basis and make sure things work, and then from
there you can move it up to a weekly basis
• This is a very powerful model for automating your launches and business.
• Start slow, and you could soon have a completely automated evergreen business
model.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now I want to talk about the weekly evergreen model. This one is pretty cool. One
of my platinum members in my platinum mastermind is using this extremely effectively
right now. He’s been doing this week after week.
We’re probably up to 20 or 30, closing in on 40 weeks that’s he’s been doing this.
Let’s say this is a week. Here’s another week. There’s another week here and another
week here.
The week can start on any day. It doesn’t matter. For illustration purposes, let’s
say it starts on Monday. We’re going to look at this from a lead stream first. We have
each week starting on Monday.
These are new leads coming in to your business. They’d be coming in through a
squeeze page. All of these leads are going on to a list this week. These are your leads for
the week.
Come Monday, they start a new sequence. This will be your launch sequence.
Then, this week, all the new lead streams coming in here start the sequence this
following Monday.
This is really easy, like with ProFollow.com or with Aweber. You can start a new
list each week. You start a new launch list. Any leads coming in go to this launch list.
Maybe in the first week, all these leads go onto launch list number one. They start
the sequence here. All of these leads here can go on to launch list number two and so on.
All the leads coming in here go on launch list number three.
The mechanics of this are simple. That’s looking at it from a lead stream. Now
let’s take a look at the same type of thing. Since each of these is a week, let’s say you’re
going to use a one week Prelaunch.
Here’s Prelaunch Content number one. Then a few days later you have Prelaunch
Content number two. Then a few days later you have Prelaunch Content number three.
Maybe right here you have the sales letter or video.
This is two to four days. This is two to three days. From here, it’s another two
days. Then, boom, your sales letter. For illustration purposes, let’s make this an eight or
nine-day Prelaunch instead of seven days.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
That means that the sales letter or video will go right here. Day one is Monday.
Maybe you go live on Tuesday. The interesting thing about this is it means you will
always have multiple launches going on.
This is launch number one. Prelaunch piece number one starts on Monday. For
launch number two, you’re releasing your first piece of Prelaunch Content right here.
You have your first piece of Prelaunch Content every Monday.
Every Tuesday, you have that sales letter from the previous week. There’s some
administrative overhead. You have to keep track of this stuff. You have to know what’s
going on.
All the leads coming in here will get a Prelaunch Content piece here and a sales
letter here. All the leads coming in here will get Prelaunch Content piece number one
here, a sales letter here and on and on. Every week you’re doing another launch. You
always have a couple of launches going on.
The beautiful thing is that you are paying for the traffic here, if you’re buying
traffic, they start the launch here and you know by here if that traffic is paid off. You’re
only waiting 10, 12 or 15 days to know if that’s paid off.
When you release a sales letter here and you have the cart open, let’s say the cart
is open 3 or 4 days, you’re closing the cart here. This is the close.
You always have a couple of launches going on. One of the things you will have to
decide is whether you want to have this thing completely automated. We’ll talk about
tools in a little bit.
You can completely automate this thing. You set up list number one. You put in
your auto responder sequence so that people get everything exactly when they’re
supposed to get it. It’s 100% automated.
You can do it that way or send out e-mails as broadcasts where you’re looking
over things. It’s your decision. For the first two, three or four times you do this, I suggest
you do not try to automate every piece.
I suggest that when you send out the e-mail for each of these pieces, the e-mail
for the sales letter and the e-mail for the cart close, you do that as a broadcast, not as an
autoresponder. You want to watch every single piece and watch the interactions.
My Platinum mastermind member who’s doing this tested different ways. When
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
he tested it, he found that when he turned it on purely automatic, his results were not as
good. It’s one of those weird things, like how does the universe know? It does seem to
know.
He went back to sending each one of these as broadcasts. It doesn’t mean you
have to do it. You can have a team member doing these types of things. The first few
times you do this, you want to do it as broadcasts.
Watch the metrics. Watch the traffic. Watch the e-mails that are coming back.
Watch the comments that are coming in. Then you adjust it.
One of the most beautiful things about this type of launch is that you can do real
testing. You can test the first piece of Prelaunch Content from week to week. You can test
the sales letter from week to week.
You can test whether the comments work better as Facebook comments or as
non- Facebook comments. You can test the design of your blog. You can test the headline
in your sales letter.
You can test pricing. You can test whatever you want. A lot of that stuff can be
hard to test in a full-blown launch. There is a big source of traffic coming in all at once. It
can be a little tough to test in that environment.
In this environment, you can test forever. Every week you get to run new tests. It
might not be the perfect test. The traffic might vary from week to week depending on
traffic sources. It’s not a perfect EB test.
You can do some great testing with this model. You can do this on a weekly basis.
You can just as easily do this on a monthly basis. The paid traffic deal gets a little
squirrely when you go with a monthly process.
You could run paid traffic into it the week before. If you’re doing it on a monthly
basis, run traffic into there the week before the start of the launch. Monthly also works
great with JVs.
It’s a very cool model. It’s not very hard to pull off. Technology-wise, you can use
the Prelaunch however you have your prelaunch build out, you can use that.
Simply start a new Aweber or ProFollow list every single week. It’s not rocket
science to pull this thing together. You can start off doing it on a monthly basis.
After you have the monthly process nailed down, you have your checklists in
